question_text: A student has a mass of 50 kg. Calculate the weight of the student on Earth, where gravitational field strength g = 10 N/kg.
explanation: Weight is calculated using the formula W = mg, where m is mass in kg and g is gravitational field strength in N/kg. So W = 50 × 10 = 500 N. Weight is measured in newtons and tells us how hard gravity is pulling on an object.
